ramp.note: 100% errors at sub-millisecond latency = connection refused (server crashed). ~33k instant rejections in 15s.

post_test_health: backend down — curl exit 7 (Connection refused) after c=2 level
notes: Heuristic mock mode (no DeepSeek key). Tool: asyncio-httpx, 15s per level. At c=1: only 4 requests completed in 15s (avg ~4.5s each) — already extremely slow, suggesting the backend was under strain from earlier test phases. At c=2: the server crashed immediately — all 33282 sub-millisecond 'responses' were instant connection-refused failures, not real completions. Baseline p50 of 2323ms at c=1 (sequential) confirms even single-request latency was elevated well above the expected sub-50ms heuristic range. The prior security-prober finding of crash at ~20 concurrent was optimistic — this run shows the server cannot sustain even c=2 after being exercised by earlier phases. Backend was confirmed down (connection refused) after the test.
brief_for_next: Load test confirmed catastrophic instability: Sentinel crashed at c=2 concurrent requests, validating and worsening the security-prober's estimate of crash at ~20 concurrent. At c=1, single-request latency had already degraded to 4500ms (from a fresh-state baseline of ~2300ms), indicating state accumulation from prior test phases. The backend was unreachable (connection refused) after the load test concluded. Root cause is the single-threaded uvicorn worker combined with in-memory SQLite write contention — there is zero concurrency headroom. Sentinel cannot handle any meaningful concurrent load in its current architecture; production deployment would require multiple uvicorn workers, a persistent database with connection pooling, and async-safe agent calls.
